title: alternanthera ficoidea var. radicans
intro: Alternanthera ficoidea var. radicans, also known as 'Creeping Joyweed', is a variety of Alternanthera ficoidea, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the amaranth family (Amaranthaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Alternanthera ficoidea var. radicans include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves and roots, then making a tea or decoction by infusing the dried plant material in hot water.
